text.tspan: Design beautiful SVG banners for your GitHub README files. Drag, drop, style and
Features • Getting Started • Usage • Templates • Tech Stack • Contributing • License
ReadMeKit is a browser-based visual editor for creating SVG banner components for GitHub README
files. Instead of writing SVG by hand or using generic design tools, you drag and drop shapes, text,
images, and lines on a live canvas, style everything with a property panel, and export a clean,
The exported SVG is optimized for GitHub rendering: sanitized attributes, optional font embedding,
dark mode support via prefers-color-scheme, and a complete README.md generator that combines your
Drag and drop rectangles, circles, text,
lines, and images on a live canvas
Export clean, GitHub-ready SVG for any README
Start from 8 pre-built layouts or create from
Reorder, lock, hide layers with drag and drop
V, R, T, O, L, I, Delete, Ctrl+Z/Y and more
The app will be available at http://localhost:5173
Output in dist/ folder, ready for Vercel, Netlify, GitHub Pages, etc.
Add shapes, text and colors to your canvas
Use the property panel to fine-tune everything
One-click export for your GitHub README
Title + subtitle on a solid background
Title + subtitle with a gradient background
Horizontal badges for your tech stack
Follows the Bulletproof React architecture pattern.
npm run dev # Start dev server (Vite)
npm run build # Type-check + production build
npm run preview # Preview production build
npm run test # Run tests once (Vitest)
npm run test:watch # Run tests in watch mode
npm run test:coverage # Tests with coverage report
npm run lint # Lint with ESLint
Contributions are welcome! Here's how to get started:
2. Create a feature branch (git checkout -b feature/my-feature)
4. Run tests (npm run test) and linting (npm run lint)
6. Push to the branch and open a Pull Request
